commit.message:
fix(expenses): fix NOT NULL constraint crash when saving manual/reduction expenses

Root cause: explicit null values for NOT NULL DEFAULT columns bypass PostgreSQL DEFAULT:
  - extraction_warnings: NOT NULL DEFAULT '{}'::text[]
  - requires_user_confirmation: NOT NULL DEFAULT false

These were set to: input.x ?? null — which inserts explicit NULL even when the
caller doesn't provide the field. PostgreSQL only uses DEFAULT when the column is
OMITTED from the INSERT, not when NULL is explicitly given.

Fix: use spread pattern to omit when value is absent, letting DB DEFAULT apply.
Same pattern already applied to cost_type in migration 122 fix.

Also:
- Add reductionError state + error display in reduction form (was silently failing)
- Clear reduction error on cancel

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: cross-namespace KopiaRepository lookup in controllers

Both the kopiabackup and pvc-watcher controllers were only looking
for KopiaRepository in the backup/PVC's namespace. When the repo
lives in a different namespace (e.g. kopia-operator) while backups
are in app namespaces (e.g. hass), the repo was never found.

Now both controllers first check the local namespace, then fall back
to listing all KopiaRepositories cluster-wide. An error is returned
if no match or multiple matches are found (ambiguous).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: auto-retarget when current target dies in combat (closes #321)

When fighting multiple creatures, defeating one left the player's
currentTarget pointing at the dead creature. The auto-attack logic
saw the dead target and defaulted to dodge instead of engaging the
next hostile.

Two-part fix:
1. Auto-attack logic (resolveTick): when currentTarget is invalid,
   call cycleTarget() to find the next valid hostile before falling
   back to dodge. Dodge only when no hostiles remain.
2. Defeat handling: after removing a defeated combatant, clear
   currentTarget for all remaining combatants that targeted them.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* feat: prototype DikuMUD zone importer script

Adds a Node.js/TypeScript converter that parses CircleMUD .wld (world/room)
files and generates Ellmud-compatible SQL migrations matching the format
established in 003_seed_zones.sql.

Features:
- Parses room vnums, names, descriptions, and exits from .wld format
- Generates deduplicated kebab-case room slugs from room names
- Maps Diku direction numbers (0-5) to Ellmud direction names
- Produces zone, zone_rooms, and zone_exits INSERTs with proper
  ON CONFLICT guards, NULLIF patterns, and cross-join VALUES
- Infers room types (entry/corridor/junction/dead_end) from exit count
- Skips cross-zone exits with clear warnings
- Maps CircleMUD exit flags to locked/hidden booleans

Includes sample output from Midgaard (58 rooms, 115 exits) for review.

Usage: npx tsx scripts/import-diku-zone.ts <file.wld> [zone-slug] [zone-name]

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: respawn at last rented inn room on death (closes #322)

Death respawn now checks the player's saved last inn location before
falling back to faction hub or the default hub (Refuge). The priority:

1. Last rented inn room (if saved via saveLastInn)
2. Faction stronghold hub
3. DEFAULT_HUB_SLUG (Refuge) as last resort

Changes:
- ZoneRoom.handlePlayerDeath() now async; queries getLastInn() first
- ROOM_SWITCH message includes targetRoomSlug in options when using inn
- Death narration says 'your rented room' for inn respawns

force-directed relaxation and subsequent refinement phases broke this
alignment. The wall-road branch pulled inside-the-west-gate 4 cells
off the main-street row.

   won't drag aligned rooms toward distant neighbours.

* feat(layout): post-BFS cardinal alignment pass

Add Phase 5c to computeLayout — a cardinal alignment pass that runs
after direction violation repair and before occlusion fix.

Problem: BFS visit order is entry-point-sensitive. When the zone
designer uses a different entry room than the zone's actual entry
(e.g. due to room type mismatch between 'entrance' and 'entry'),
east/west-connected rooms can end up at different y levels, creating
visual right-angles where straight corridors should appear.

Solution: Build union-find groups for E/W exits (rooms that must
share the same y) and N/S exits (rooms that must share the same x).
For each group with misaligned coordinates, batch-shift all outlier
rooms plus their perpendicular subtrees to the majority coordinate.
Accept the shift only if the global layout score improves.

The cascade-shift approach preserves north/south topology: when an
E/W-misaligned room moves to match its row, its entire N/S subtree
moves with it, keeping wall-road correctly south of the gate.

Test: new entry-point independence test verifies E/W alignment holds
across 5 different BFS entry points on the same graph.

* Replace row exchange with true grid expansion for crossing fixes

Phase 5e/9 now resolves edge crossings by inserting blank rows/columns
rather than swapping existing rows. For each H×V crossing:

1. Shift a partition of the grid to create a gap OUTSIDE the crossing
   edge's span (e.g. shift rooms above V's top by -1)
2. Move the crossing edge's alignment group into the gap

This genuinely adds space to the grid at crossing points. The partition
shift preserves all internal alignment; only the moved group's jump

* fix: resolve admin spawn-creature crash from flat-to-nested template mismatch

The creature content store (PgCreatureDefinitionsStore) returns a flat
ContentEntity with top-level fields (maxHp, attack, minCount, etc.)
but CreatureManager.spawnSingleCreature() expects a nested CreatureTemplate
with stats: { maxHp, attack, ... } and spawnRules: { minCount, ... }.

The unsafe 'as unknown as CreatureTemplate' cast masked this shape mismatch,
causing template.stats to be undefined and crashing on template.stats.maxHp.

- Add contentEntityToCreatureTemplate() helper that maps flat → nested shape
- Replace both unsafe casts in the spawn-creature and spawn-creature (zone) paths
- Update test fixture to use flat ContentEntity shape matching real store output

* fix: spawned creatures now appear in admin room list for static zones

The roomOccupancy memo in LiveRoomDetail prioritized zoneData.rooms
(from a separate /zones/:slug API call fetched once at mount) over
roomGraphRooms (from the same /rooms/:roomId response as creatures).

because their currentRoomId didn't match any key in the occupancy map.

  over zoneData.rooms (separate, potentially stale fetch)
- Add defensive auto-creation of map entries for creatures whose
  currentRoomId doesn't match any known room, preventing silent drops

commit.message:
fix: rehydrate stale randoms data without page reload

When the Ships tab loads stale randoms data (>24h old), the backend
serves the cached result immediately and dispatches an async Celery
refresh. Previously the component never re-fetched, so the stale
timestamp persisted until a manual page reload.

Now RandomsSVG detects a stale X-Randoms-Updated-At timestamp and
schedules up to 4 re-fetches at 6s intervals with cache-busting keys,
picking up the Celery-refreshed data as soon as it lands. The retry
loop stops early once a fresh timestamp arrives.

On each runner startup, CleanupStaleTaskFilesAsync scans the repo's

commit.message:
PyCyphal v2 (#379)

This is the new implementation that was first announced in https://github.com/OpenCyphal/pycyphal/issues/351 almost a year ago.

While Cyphal/CAN is wire-compatible between the two versions, the API is completely different, and so is the design philosophy. PyCyphal v2 does not attempt to be a one-stop shop for Cyphal, rather it focuses on connectivity only, intentionally omitting application-level concerns. As a result, the new API is a tiny fraction of the old.
